Persons

Jiva Goswami, Rupa Goswami, Sanatan Goswami, Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Nityanada Prabhu

Themes

New year Resolutions - becoming the servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord

Lecture Transcription

Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu’s Sankirtana movement

One of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan…the six Goswamis of Vrindavan were the confidential associates of Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Their particular mission is most significant to the lives of each and every one of us, because it is by their mercy that the world is able to understand the glorious mission of Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu. It was they that enunciated and immortalized the life and teachings of Sri Caitanya. As we know, Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu through the forty-eight years, in which he performed his earthly lila, he personally emphasized in his own life an example - the congregational chanting of the holy name of Sri Krishna. Every day, he would take his devotees to the streets of Navadwip and later on to the streets of Jagannath Puri. With their arms raised, they would dance in a most beautiful manner and in a sweet and melodious voice. He would lead them all on, in chanting the holy names - Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare/Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare.

But throughout his life, Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu only wrote eight verses. But, he empowered the six Goswamis to take the essence of his teachings, which is, that the goal of life is pure bhakti, pure devotion to the Personality of Godhead. That this human form of life is not meant for sense enjoyment. Why? Because we are not these bodies, which are growing old, getting diseased and are about to die. Instead of wasting this valuable human life decorating these dead bodies, we should obtain the real happiness that human life affords us by awakening pure bhakti, pure devotion to Krishna from within our hearts. And in the age of Kali Yuga, there is one process, which is simple and available to everyone, and that is the chanting of the holy name of Lord Hari. Now, of course, this is a very simple formula. But it is explained that it is the essence of all the Vedic knowledge. And therefore, Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu specifically instructed Rupa Goswami and Sanatana Goswami to carefully and analytically study all the principal Vedic literatures and prove how the essence of all knowledge is pure devotional service to Sri Krishna, and how the only means of attaining that in the age of Kali Yuga is through the chanting of the holy names.

Mahaprabhu instructing Srila Rupa & Sanatana Goswami

So Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, when he was at Prayag, Allahabad…he instructed Srila Rupa Goswami for fifteen days at the Dasasvamedha Ghat. In fact, for those of you who are interested, the very exact place where Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu and Srila Rupa Goswami sat for fifteen days is still present today. Last year when we went to the Kumbha-mela at the Triveni Sangam, we visited that holy place. It is only a few minutes walk from the holy confluence of the Ganges, the Yamuna and the Sarasvati. And there the footprints of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu are present. And it is designated that sitting on this very spot, the Lord instructed Rupa Goswami. Very close to the same time in the holy city of Kashi for one month, Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu instructed Srila Sanatana Goswami. Very systematically he explained the goal of life and how to obtain it. And then by his causeless mercy to all living beings, he sent Rupa and Sanatana to the holy city of Vrindavan. Of course, at those days it was not a city. It was just the jungle. There was hardly even a few very saintly persons who were living in that jungle. And the divine places of Sri Krishna’s lila were practically lost to the people in general. So Lord Gaura Sundara instructed Rupa and Sanatana that, “you go to Vraja dham and there I have a great mission for you. You study all the Vedic literatures and make it known to the world the essence of my teachings. Also, I will empower you with the divine vision to perceive the holy lila of Krishna, that Nitya lila, which is eternally manifesting in Vrindavan and you excavate those holy places where Krishna performed his various pastimes, make them into holy places of pilgrimage, so that people from all over the world can come to take shelter of the holy dham, to worship the Lord's lila, the Lord's dham, and to become purified. Also you establish beautiful temples and establish the proper standard of how to worship the deity of Lord Sri Krishna and Sri Radharani with great love.” And Mahaprabhu Sri Caitanya told them that, “I also want you to establish through your teachings and through your lives, the perfect behaviour for a vaisnava devotee of the Lord and the proper etiquette in which vaisnavas should treat one another.” At that time, Rupa Goswami was traveling with the third brother. There were three brothers - the eldest brother was Sanatana, then there was Rupa and there was another brother of the name Anupam. These brothers went to Vrindavan but on the order of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Rupa and Anupam came back to Jagannath Puri by foot to visit Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. And on the way, Anupam contracted a sickness and he left this world to re-enter into the spiritual world of Vaikuntha. But Anupam had one son. He was a very young boy at the time. But he was not an ordinary boy. In fact, it is described that when Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu descended onto this earth, along with him he brought his eternal associates from Goloka Vrindavan. Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu is the combined forms of Sri Sri Radha and Krishna. And just as he descended, he also brought his elder brother Balaram in the form of Lord Nityananda, Narada Muni appeared as Srivas Thakur, Karanodakashayi Vishnu appeared as Sri Advaita acharya. The intimate expansion of the pleasure potency of Sri Radha Rani appeared as Gadadhar Pandit. There is a book by the son of Sivananda Sen - Kavi Karnapura. It is called the Gaura Ganoddesha Dipika. And there describes all of the various associates of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu and who they were in Lord Sri Krishna’s lila. So this son of Anupam, he was none other than one of the principal manjari gopis, the most confidential maid servant of Radharani. That most illustrious servant of Sri Radha...Sri Radhika, Vilas manjari gopi descended into this world and took the form of Srila Jiva Goswami. It is described that after Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu left this world, just after the final pastimes of the Lord, Srila Jiva Goswami hearing the news of Lord Gaura Sundara’s departure became overwhelmed with separation from the Lord. It was unbearable. Just like when in this world we love someone very dearly, separation from that person is like a burning fire within our heart. But understand that this is the limited perverted reflection of real love. But the soul's divine love for Sri Krishna is the most super excellent, perfect and purest form of all love.

Jiva Goswami’s intense feelings of separation from Lord Caitanya

Therefore, Jiva Goswami when he heard the news, that Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu had left this world knowing that he would never ever again be able to see him, he became like a madman in separation. He was weeping, he was crying. He did not know whether he would live or die. He did not know whether he should live or die. He was delirious. So he decided that he would leave his home in Bengal and go to the holy land of Navadwip, because there Lord Sri Nityananda Prabhu was still present. So it is described that as Jiva Goswami was walking down the road, he would fall down, he would faint, he would cry, he would get up, he would walk, he would fall down, he would cry, he would faint, unconscious and conscious. He was constantly surfacing and sinking into the ocean of transcendental separation from the lord. And alas, when he came to the holy land of Sri Navadwip dham, he inquired where is that most merciful and munificent of all personalities, Sri Nityananda Prabhu. He who out of his infinite kindness even delivered the two most sinful of all demons, Jagai and Madhai, where is he to be found? At that time, Lord Nityananda, it is described he was with some of his associates sitting under a tree. He could feel by his divine transcendental cognizance that Jiva Goswami had come. So he immediately called for Jiva Goswami to come to him. And the service of Nityananda found Sri Jiva. And when Jiva Goswami saw the lotus feet of Lord Nityananda, he became enraptured in divine happiness. He fell at those lotus Feet crying tears of love. He began to offer beautiful prayers full of devotion. Jiva Goswami, with his own lotus hands he lifted… I mean Nityananda prabhu with his own lotus hands he lifted Jiva Goswami and embraced him. And then it was agreed that Lord Nityananda would personally take Jiva Goswami to all of the holy places of Sri Navadwip dham and reveal to him the divine lila of Sri Gaura Hari.

Lord Nityananda revealing the pastimes of Lord Caitanya to Srila Jiva Goswami

So for many many days, as Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur writes in his Sri Navadwip Mahatmya, Lord Nityananda Prabhu described the most intimate lila of Sri Caitanya, place after place after place to the great pleasure of Srila Jiva Goswami. It is described that the love of a devotee attracts the mercy of Krishna. Although Krishna is the supreme, most attractive and he is attracting the hearts of all living beings, he is eternally attracted by the devotion of his servants. So as Lord Nityananda and Jiva Goswami were going from place to place, when they came to the place of Srivas-angam, the place where each and every night, Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu would gather his associates and they would have nocturnal kirtan, they would chant, they would dance throughout the night. As Lord Nityananda was describing these transcendental pastimes and chanting the glories of the Lord, Jiva Goswami was so anxious out of love to see this wonderful lila of the Lord that right before his eyes, Lord Nityananda began to dance. And as he was dancing Jiva Goswami perceived that Caitanya Mahaprabhu appeared and began to dance with Lord Nityananda. Then he heard mridangas and kartals and he heard tumultuous voices and he looked around and there was Gadadhar Pandit, there was Swarup Damodar, there was Vakreshwar Pandit, there was Srivas Thakur, there was all the associates of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu with tears of love in their eyes leap into the air with their arms raised chanting, hare Kṛṣṇa hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare Rāma hare Rāma Rāma Rāma hare hare. Because it is a fact, in the holy dham, the transcendental pastimes of the Lord are eternally taking place. And by the mercy of the great souls, they can reveal this divine lila right before our eyes. In this way Jiva Goswami, all of his desires were fulfilled. He fell on the ground again unconscious in very sublime ecstasy. And then he awoke, and he took part in the great kirtan, and it went on, it appeared that it was going on for thousands of days of Brahma. It appeared like a never ending kirtan. It appeared that the holy names were like waves and waves and waves just coming from the ocean with no end. And then Caitanya Mahaprabhu and all of his associates disappeared from his vision and Lord Nityananda Prabhu explained to Jiva Goswami that the Lord's pastimes are always manifesting for one whose heart is pure.

Jiva Goswami becomes a great scholar and leader of the devotees throughout India

So after this most famous and worshipable parikram of Sri Mayapur and Navadwip dham, Lord Nityananda Prabhu placed his lotus feet on the head of Srila Jiva Goswami and he told him, “Sri Jiva, Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu has given Vrindavan to your family. Your uncles Rupa and Sanatana are now living in Vrindavan and you should join them and there spend the rest of your life assisting them in their spiritual mission in the service of the Lord.” He said, “But first, you should go to Varanasi, and you should study Sanskrit under a pure devotee of the Lord of the name Madhusudan Vacaspati. And then you continue forward to Sri Vrindavan dham. So, Jiva Goswami taking leave from Lord Nityananda Prabhu, he went to Kashi and there he studied and became the foremost scholar on all the earth. And then he proceeded to the holy land of Braj. And there he came in the association of Sri Rupa and Sri Sanatana. And together along with Raghunath Das Goswami, Raghunath Bhatta Goswami and Gopal Bhatta Goswami - these six most illustrious personalities immortalized for all the world, the life and teachings of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu. It is described that anyone who claims to be a follower of Lord Caitanya must accept the instructions and the guidance of the six Goswamis of Vrindavana. They are our guides. They are the gurus for all of us. After Rupa and Sanatana established in Vrindavan the mission of Sri Caitanya, there were so many devotees, there were tens and thousands of devotees all over Vrindavan, Bengal, Orissa, in fact, all over India. And they all worshipped Rupa and Sanatana as the supreme gurus of their life. Although there were many associates who were initiating disciples, everyone accepted Rupa and Sanatana as the leaders of the mission. You see, in those days, there was no envy amongst the devotees. We read in the pastimes of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu’s servants, in such books as the Bhakti Ratnakar how there were so many great gurus in those days. Most all of them were eternal associates, gopis of vrindavan, who had descended down to this world to assist the lord with his mission. But all of them accepted Rupa and Sanatana as their leaders. Is not that there was competition that my guru is…. why your guru is better than mine and why my guru is better than yours? No, no no. This is all material thinking. All the acaryas, all the paramahansas, they all bowed to the feet of Rupa and Sanatana. And when Rupa and Sanatana, when they were called back to the spiritual world by Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, at that time Jiva Goswami, like the self manifesting sun rising in the eastern horizon, he was accepted by every acarya, by every paramahamsa, by every guru, by every devotee, as the leader of the entire mission of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

All the millions of disciples in Bengal, in Orissa, in Vrindavan, in South India and all around, wherever Caitanya Mahaprabhu went and preached, wherever his servants spread his message, they all accepted Jiva Goswami as the leader. Jiva Goswami amongst the six Goswamis was considered the greatest of all philosophers. Even great souls such as Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami who is the author of Caitanya Caritamrita, he was the humble servant of the servant of the servants of Sri Jiva Goswami.

In fact, Srila Prabhupada and all other acaryas in the Gaudiya Vaishnav tradition, they accept the works of Jiva Goswami as the most pristine philosophical masterpieces ever revealed on this earth. Sometimes, people have a misconception that the devotees of the lord, they just like to dance and they like to chant and they like to eat too much prasad. They are very sentimental. But yoga entails much gyan and philosophical expertise. But anyone who was once even seeing the works of Jiva Goswami, all of these misconceptions are destroyed. How masterly, Lord Sri Krishna has empowered him to establish with the ultimate authority, the most analytical philosophy explaining the supreme position of bhakti and the supreme position of the Supreme Personality of Godhead Sri Krishna and the supreme abode of Vrindavan. Therefore Jiva Goswami is our guru, he is our master, he is our leader. And on this most holy and auspicious day of his disappearance, it is our very very auspicious fortune that we are allowed to remember him, we are allowed to worship his lotus feet, and we are allowed to appreciate the great fortune we have in being the servant of the servant of the servant of his most illustrious servants. That is the supreme benediction in our life. The right to serve the lotus feet of such great souls. And it is very auspicious arrangement of Lord Sri Krishna, that tonight being New Year's Eve, we can begin the year by remembering and worshiping the lotus feet of one of the great servants of Lord Sri Krishna. There is no benediction superior than the opportunity to become the servant of the servant.

Great souls never ask the Lord for any material bendictions

And all the great souls in the Vedic literatures we find, never do they ask the Lord for any material benediction. They never asked the lord for elevation to heavenly planets. They never asked the lord for good health. They never asked the lord for wealth, for nice family life. They never even asked the lord for mukti - liberation. Seldom will they ever even ask the lord for elevation to vaikuntha, the spiritual world. But there's only one prayer of the real genuine devotees of the lord. And what is that prayer? “My Lord, please, with your mercy, if you desire, bless me that I may always remember you by becoming the servant of the servant of the servant of your servants.” Less intelligent persons desire other benedictions but the real devotees of the lord aspire only for the service of the vaisnavas. “chadiya vaisnava-seva nistara payeche keba”- Narrottam das Thakur has prayed, that no one has ever understood Krishna without becoming the humble servant of the vaishnavas, of the devotees, of the lord. So, of course, this was the mood of Jiva Goswami. This is the mood of all the servants of Jiva Goswami and it is customary on New Year's Eve to make resolutions for the New Year, is it not?

Krishnaizing our birthdays

New Year's resolutions… I remember Prabhupada said “Resolution, revolution, dissolution it is all useless.” But of course we could dovetail that propensity. Devotees know how to Krishnaize everything, and make everything auspicious. So we can make a new year's resolution. I remember Srila Prabhupada also said – “What is this celebrating of birthdays? It is all useless.”[Laughs] Because of your bad karma, you've been forced by the laws of nature to be crunched out of the womb of your mother. And you're celebrating that? Happy birthday wasn't very happy… Was not very happy when it was going on. Your mother was screaming in pain and you were crying, ah? That was… most of us don't remember but we don't want to remember. Have you ever seen birth? What's happy about birth, my God? It's torture! It's all due to our karma, that we are forced to take another birth. The next step after death is rebirth. Happy birthday. But a devotee can make it a happy birthday, ah? How? By celebrating it, by giving gifts to Krishna and his devotees. If you do it like that, then you can have nice birthday. Then it is Krishna conscious.

Just like some months ago, one of our devotees, he had his son - his birthday party at Krishna’s temple. He's sitting here this evening. It was a very, very glorious occasion. I, all the friends came, was like a birthday. They don't know the difference the children, but it was different, because they serve prasad to all the devotees. They prepared a nice feast for Lord Sri Krishna, and Krishna was the centre of everything. The whole atmosphere was surcharged with Krishna. Any occasion to glorify Krishna we accept, whether you want to call it a birthday, whether you want to call it a new year's eve party, whether you want to call it a christmas party, whether you want to call it a funeral, whether you want to call it anything you want to call it, as long as you're glorifying Krishna, we accept it's bonafide, it's authorized and it's wonderful. But if it's not in the glorification of Krishna, it's all just “punah punas carvita carvananam”- chewing the chewed, ah? If you like birthday parties so much that means you have to keep going birth after birth after birth after birth after birth. It's not the goal of life. [Laughter] The goal of life is no more birth, ah?

Truth about new year resolutions

So people are taking…. making their new year's resolutions. I will from now on only smoke two packs of cigarettes a day instead of two and a half packs of cigarettes a day. I will only get drunk on Fridays and Saturday nights. In America, these are the kinds of revolution… resolutions there are… These …what I'm saying are very severe austerities for the Americans. I will try not to have sex with anyone but my wife this year. These are the resolutions they make in the west, I have seen. And various business resolutions. But on this night, we can follow in the footsteps of Srila Jiva Goswami and his servants and we can make a resolution. And what should that resolution be? It should be the prayer of every great devotee. That I will not criticize any Vaishnava, that I will strive to worship the feet of Krishna and his devotees. And I will make my life and soul the service of the servants of the servants of the servants of Krishna. This is the greatest blessing and this is… there is no resolution that will please Krishna, more than this.

And it is described in Bhagavatam [SB 1.2.13] “Samsiddhir Hari Tossanam”. And everything we do is meant to please Krishna. And Krishna says “Mad bhakta pujabhyadhikar”- there's nothing that pleases me more than serving my devotees. Anyone can find fault with devotees, but only a great soul can humble themselves and become the servant. In fact, we should become the servants of all living entities. Every human being, every animal, every specie of life, we should become the humble servant. We should perceive god within the heart of everyone. But this is a very high elevated platform. But it begins by serving those who are surrendering their lives or at least attempting to surrender their lives to the lord. You know, being humble is a very difficult thing in this age. We are conditioned to want to be the master, to want to be the controller, to compete with others, to want to outdo others. We are conditioned to be envious. So it is a very bold stance and a very, very glorious act on our part to make this new year's resolution. My dear lord… actually our resolution is more of a prayer. My dear lord, I pray for your mercy. Give me the strength to resolve within my mind that on this day, on this holy day of Jiva Goswami, I will make the resolution to become the servant of the servant of your servants, to assist your devotees in any way I can, to humble myself before your devotees in any way I can. One who is seeing in this light will never have any difficulties in his spiritual life. But as soon as we forget our real position of the servant of the servant, there are so many complexities and so many difficulties that will come and bewilder our minds, ah? So of course, you know how the karmis do it, I don't know if you know, but I know.

After you see on New Year's Eve, everyone makes the resolutions. And then after they make their resolutions, the resolution is confirmed by taking their glasses of alcohol and clashing them together and then drinking it and becoming intoxicated, ah? It is called a toast. You have heard? they say all right, we have made our resolutions and they all held up their glasses, they all state their resolutions and then they click their glasses together, and then they drink. And then they make another resolution, lek lek (Maharaja making sound) and they drink, and then another resolution, tin-tin they drink. And somebody else makes the resolution. Ting, let... And pretty soon they're completely drunk. And then they come home and their children, little children, they usually left alone on New Year’s Eve. Trying to imitate the parents, the children, they snuck and found their parent’s alcohol and trying to imitate them, they all got drunk. But then they're pretending to be asleep so the parents don't know it. They watch for the car, then they run up, they're coming, they're coming. Then the next morning, the whole family is intimately together in the bathroom vomiting… that is the culmination of their so called material life, simply vomiting. So I wasn't intending on describing these past times of… Is this the way it is in India? In America that's the way it is in every house.

Real intoxication and prayer for the new year

So first we make our resolutions and then we become intoxicated. We can do like that too. But our intoxication is far more intoxicating. And the next day, there's no vomiting, we may have sore legs. We may have calluses on our feet. So there is some after effects. But the intoxication is eternally purifying to our heart, to our soul. So, let us have a few minutes of prayer. Making our resolutions for the coming year in the service of Lord Sri Krishna and his devotees, praying that we may… can reconfirm the vows that we have taken, to follow the principles of Krishna consciousness, to attentively chant the holy names of Krishna and especially to increase the quality of the humility of our service to the Lord.

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
